campaign_notes: Account permanently blocked by Wikipedia admin Asilvering on 2026-05-19. Block confirmed still active (expiry: infinity) as of 2026-06-12 API check. No editing possible. Do not attempt to create a new account (block evasion). Do not edit from alternate IP. Legitimate path forward is human editor with no COI, or wait for organic Wikipedia coverage. All three external links on Tove Lo, Little Simz, and Tems were removed as of the 2026-05-25 session check.

decline_history.reason: Bio sourcing: unfamiliar referencing style (WP:REFB); YouTube and Vimeo not reliable sources

open_reviewer_notes.note: In 20 years of editing here I have not come across this referencing style and YouTube and Vimeo are not considered reliable sources
Notability does not hinge on the existence of the subjects work. Need media coverage about him in newspapers, magazines, books. YouTube, Vimeo, self-published websites, blogs not acceptable.

prepared_revision.description: Clean revision ready but blocked from posting
changes: Converted all sfn+sfnRef citations to standard <ref> tags
Removed all YouTube citations (zero YouTube/Vimeo sources remain)
Removed Wikipedia self-citation (Dine Alone Records article)
Trimmed article to 9,996 bytes (from 45,425 bytes) keeping only RS-supported content
Added AFC submission template for resubmission

All remaining sources: LBBOnline, shots.net, adobo Magazine, Stereogum, VideoStatic, EDM Canada, Newswire.ca, Ad Age, EARMILK, The Music, Young Director Award, OnepointFour, The One Club, SHOOTonline, Campaign US, Exclaim!

targets.title: Amos Le Blanc (film director)

assessed_2026-05-25.assessment: JPEGMAFIA already has a Wikipedia article. No new article needed. Album article for EXPERIMENTAL RAP could be a candidate but requires third-party sourcing from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, NME, etc. Not actionable with blocked account.

assessed_2026-05-25.article: Bleachers and everyone for ten minutes
assessed_2026-05-25.assessment: Bleachers and Jack Antonoff both have Wikipedia articles. Album article for everyone for ten minutes may be warranted given commercial and critical profile but requires third-party sourcing and an unblocked account.

assessed_2026-05-26.article: Aja Monet and the color of rain
assessed_2026-05-26.assessment: Aja Monet has an existing Wikipedia article. Her new album the color of rain (released May 22, 2026 on Drink Sum Wtr) does not yet have a Wikipedia album article. The artonly piece references AllMusic (score 90), Carnegie Hall performance, and notable collaborators (Meshell Ndegeocello, Ambrose Akinmusire, Brandee Younger, Mick Jenkins, Vic Mensa, Ganavya). Album is 21 days old as of 2026-06-12. Monitor for Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, NPR Music, Guardian coverage. Not actionable: account blocked.

assessed_2026-05-26.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Ed O'Brien pageid=273486; EOB (musician) pageid=63179030
assessed_2026-05-26.assessment: Ed O'Brien has an existing Wikipedia article. EOB (musician) has an existing Wikipedia article. Blue Morpho (album) already has a Wikipedia article. No new article needed.

assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: MISSING (confirmed 2026-06-12; Traumprinz, Phillip Lauer, Tuff City Kids all absent from English Wikipedia)
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Phillip Lauer (Traumprinz) is a German electronic producer from the Cologne scene, also known as Prince of Denmark and one half of Tuff City Kids with Gerd Janson. Life is the fifth Traumprinz album on the All Possible Worlds imprint. No English Wikipedia articles exist for Traumprinz, Phillip Lauer, or Tuff City Kids. The artist has significant underground credibility but primarily exists within specialist electronic music press (Resident Advisor, Wire, etc.) rather than mainstream RS. Monitor for RS coverage. Not actionable: account blocked and notability would need verification against WP:GNG via RS search.

assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Lowertown (band) pageid=72035125 (last touched 2026-05-23, 6848 bytes). Article covers through debut album I Love to Lie (2022). Does not mention Ugly Duckling Union.
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Lowertown (band) has an existing Wikipedia article. Their second album Ugly Duckling Union (May 2026, Summer Shade / Run For Cover Records) received Stereogum Album of the Week and NPR Music top 5 of its release week. The band article is outdated and does not mention the new album. An Ugly Duckling Union album article would be supportable given Stereogum and NPR Music coverage, but account is blocked. The band article itself could be updated with the new album when an unblocked editor is available.
assessed_2026-05-28.note: Lowertown band article (pageid 72035125) needs updating to include Ugly Duckling Union. Album article creation viable if more RS coverage accumulates.

assessed_2026-05-28.article: Mexican Institute of Sound and Meridian Brothers / Ruido Tovar
assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: Mexican Institute of Sound EXISTS pageid=13468391. Meridian Brothers EXISTS pageid=76600887. Rigo Tovar (historical figure referenced in title) EXISTS pageid=1668428.
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Both collaborating artists have Wikipedia articles. The album Ruido Tovar is a 2026 release on Ansonia Records featuring a guest appearance by Beck. No Wikipedia album article exists. Given the profile of the collaborators (Camilo Lara / MIS and Eblis Alvarez / Meridian Brothers are both notable enough for existing Wikipedia articles) and the Beck feature, an album article may be viable if third-party RS coverage from Pitchfork, Bandcamp Daily, Resident Advisor, The Wire, or NPR Music materializes. Not actionable: account blocked.

assessed_2026-05-30.article: 6LACK and Love Is the New Gangsta
assessed_2026-05-30.wikipedia_album_article: EXISTS Love Is the New Gangsta pageid=83182365
assessed_2026-05-30.assessment: 6LACK has an existing Wikipedia article and Love Is the New Gangsta already has a Wikipedia album article (released May 22, 2026 on LVRN/Interscope). No new article needed.

assessed_2026-05-30.article: Future Islands and From a Hole in the Floor to a Fountain of Youth
assessed_2026-05-30.assessment: Future Islands has an existing Wikipedia article. Their 20th anniversary rarities and B-sides collection From a Hole in the Floor to a Fountain of Youth (released May 22, 2026 on 4AD, 20 tracks including material never previously on streaming) has no Wikipedia album article. Given Future Islands' established notability, 4AD label, and the significance of a 20-year retrospective, an album article is a viable future candidate. Requires Pitchfork, Stereogum, NME, or Consequence coverage. Not actionable: account blocked.

assessed_2026-05-30.wikipedia_album_article: EXISTS Inferno (Boards of Canada album) pageid=83012220
assessed_2026-05-30.assessment: Boards of Canada has an existing Wikipedia article and their fifth studio album Inferno (released May 29, 2026 on Warp, first album in 13 years) already has a Wikipedia album article. No new article needed.

assessed_2026-05-30.article: Kurt Vile and Philadelphia's Been Good to Me
assessed_2026-05-30.wikipedia_album_article: EXISTS Philadelphia's Been Good to Me pageid=83211808
assessed_2026-05-30.assessment: Kurt Vile has an existing Wikipedia article and his tenth studio album Philadelphia's Been Good to Me (released May 29, 2026 on Verve Forecast) already has a Wikipedia album article. No new article needed.

assessed_2026-05-30.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Balming Tiger pageid=69527142 (last revid 1356791998, touched 2026-05-29)
assessed_2026-05-30.assessment: Balming Tiger has an existing Wikipedia article (updated as recently as 2026-05-29). Their second full album Gongbu (released May 19, 2026, on their own infrastructure) has no Wikipedia album article. The album received NPR Music New Music Friday coverage. Balming Tiger's notable international profile, including the Sexy Nukim single with RM from BTS, makes them a recognized collective. An album article for Gongbu would require sustained RS coverage beyond a single NPR roundup mention. Monitor for Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, or NME dedicated reviews. Not actionable: account blocked and coverage volume insufficient at this date.

assessed_2026-06-10.wikipedia_album_article: EXISTS Cry Baby (Vince Staples album) pageid=83119332
assessed_2026-06-10.assessment: Vince Staples has an existing Wikipedia article and Cry Baby (released June 5, 2026 on Section Eight Arthouse / Loma Vista Recordings, his seventh studio album and first on his own imprint) already has a Wikipedia album article. No new article needed.

assessed_2026-06-10.article: Jalen Ngonda and Doctrine of Love
assessed_2026-06-10.assessment: Jalen Ngonda has an existing Wikipedia article. His sophomore album Doctrine of Love (released June 5, 2026 on Daptone Records) has no Wikipedia album article. The album is 7 days old as of 2026-06-12. Jalen Ngonda was named to Rolling Stone's Future of Music list after his debut. With a confirmed existing artist article and a Daptone release, an album article is a viable future candidate if coverage grows. Not actionable: account blocked and album too new to have sufficient RS coverage depth. Monitor for Pitchfork, NPR Music, Stereogum, and Consequence dedicated reviews.

assessed_2026-06-12.assessment: Bedouine (Azniv Korkejian) has an existing Wikipedia article. Her fourth studio album Neon Summer Skin (her first for Thirty Tigers) has no Wikipedia album article. The artonly piece highlights the album's biographical weight: Korkejian traveled to Saudi Arabia to visit her parents before their planned relocation to Armenia, giving the record its emotional center. Production by Jonathan Rado (Foxygen, Weyes Blood). Collaborators include Guy Syffert and the D'Addario brothers from The Lemon Twigs. The album is 1 day old as of the artonly post date. An album article would require dedicated coverage from Pitchfork, NPR Music, Stereogum, The Guardian, or AllMusic. Not actionable: account blocked and album too new for RS coverage assessment.

assessed_2026-06-12.article: Navy Blue (Sage Elsesser) and Sir Render
assessed_2026-06-12.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Sage Elsesser pageid=61099853 (22181 bytes). Navy Blue (rapper) pageid=65204020 is a redirect to Sage Elsesser.
assessed_2026-06-12.assessment: Sage Elsesser (Navy Blue) has an existing Wikipedia article. His third studio album Sir Render on his Freedom Sounds imprint has no Wikipedia album article. The album completes a trilogy beginning with Song of Sage: Post Panic! (2021) and The Sword and the Soaring (2022), functioning as a prequel addressing the spiritual and personal crisis that preceded those records. Features include a posthumous appearance by Ka (Brownsville rapper who died September 2024), Armand Hammer, and Earl Sweatshirt. The Ka posthumous feature on the track Circa carries particular significance given Ka's passing. Album is 1 day old as of the artonly post date. An album article would require dedicated coverage from Pitchfork, Stereogum, The Wire, Bandcamp Daily, or Consequence of Sound. Not actionable: account blocked and album too new for RS coverage assessment.

strategy.priority_1: BLOCKED. Amos Le Blanc draft cannot be edited. Account permanently blocked.
strategy.priority_2: Find 3rd-party coverage of artonly.io before attempting artonly article (check after 2026-08-01)
strategy.priority_3: External links on Tove Lo, Little Simz, Tems all removed as of 2026-05-25 check. No Wikipedia presence remains.
strategy.priority_4: If Wikipedia article for Amos Le Blanc is a goal, engage a human Wikipedia editor with no COI through a disclosed paid editing request
strategy.priority_5: Monitor Aja Monet the color of rain album for third-party coverage growth. Reassess Wikipedia album article viability 2026-08-01.
strategy.priority_6: Lowertown (band) Wikipedia article outdated. Ugly Duckling Union not mentioned. Update when account unblocked or human editor available.
strategy.priority_7: Monitor Traumprinz / Phillip Lauer for RS coverage. No English Wikipedia article exists. Potential future candidate.
strategy.priority_8: Monitor Ruido Tovar (MIS x Meridian Brothers) for Pitchfork/NPR/Wire coverage. Album article viable if coverage grows.
strategy.priority_9: Monitor Future Islands From a Hole in the Floor to a Fountain of Youth for Pitchfork/Stereogum/NME coverage. Album article viable given artist notability and 4AD label. Check 2026-08-01.
strategy.priority_10: Monitor Balming Tiger Gongbu for dedicated RS reviews beyond NPR roundup mention. Album article viable if sustained coverage materializes. Check 2026-08-01.
strategy.priority_11: Monitor Jalen Ngonda Doctrine of Love for Pitchfork/NPR Music/Stereogum/Consequence dedicated reviews. Artist has existing Wikipedia article (pageid 80307165) and Daptone pedigree. Album article viable if RS coverage depth confirms WP:NALBUM. Album released June 5, 2026. Check 2026-08-01.
strategy.priority_12: Monitor Bedouine Neon Summer Skin for Pitchfork/NPR Music/Stereogum/Guardian dedicated reviews. Artist has existing Wikipedia article (pageid 61061559). Fourth studio album on Thirty Tigers. Check 2026-08-01.
strategy.priority_13: Monitor Navy Blue (Sage Elsesser) Sir Render for Pitchfork/Stereogum/The Wire/Bandcamp Daily coverage. Artist has existing Wikipedia article (pageid 61099853). Third studio album on Freedom Sounds. Posthumous Ka feature and Armand Hammer/Earl Sweatshirt features raise profile. Check 2026-08-01.
strategy.source_quality: LBBOnline, shots.net, The One Club = strong. Campaign US, SHOOTonline = strong. Stereogum, NPR Music, Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Wire, Resident Advisor = strong. IMVDb = no longer acceptable. IMDb = never. YouTube/Vimeo = never.
